steps.title: Identify the key disagreement
steps.content: Text 1 claims the selection obviously delivers awareness and tourism benefits. Text 2's “However” introduces a competing view: benefits aren't automatic—cities need intentional marketing strategies, or visitors won't even be aware.

steps.title: Find the choice expressing Text 2's response
steps.content:
Choice B concedes that the city “may have benefited” but emphasizes that tourism gains require active marketing and awareness—precisely what Text 2 argues.

The other three address different matters: who deserves selection credit (A), timeliness of the honor (C), and benefits beyond tourism (D).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
